title: A protocol for a systematic review of evidence on consumer preferences and willingness to pay for insects as food and feed ingredient
author: Nikravech, Mariam
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: This is a systematic review of studies measuring consumer preferences and Willingness-To-Pay (WTP) for food products made from edible insects, or those derived from animals fed with insects, employing diverse preference elicitation methods. It aims to summarize the evidence regarding current levels of consumer demand for food products made with edible insects, or those derived of animals fed with insects. A dedicated protocol has been prepared according to the following guidelines : Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic reviews and Meta-Analysis Protocols (PRISMA-P): https://doi.org/gcpzzq Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic reviews and Meta-Analyses literature Search extension (PRISMA-S): https://doi.org/gh2z2k The protocol outlines the aims of the review, inclusion criteria, search and screening strategies, and data extraction, critical appraisal and evidence synthesis and reporting procedures, forming the basis for this registration. The protocol can also be found here: https://osf.io/9exu6
